## input

For headset-targeted VR apps, XR controller or hand input should be first-class.
Keyboard/mouse should be a browser fallback, not the only supported input path.

Prefer the stateful XR input API over raw `Gamepad` array indexing:

```typescript
import { InputComponent } from '@iwsdk/core';

const rightGamepad = world.input.xr.gamepads.right;
const triggerDown = rightGamepad?.getButtonDown(InputComponent.Trigger);
const squeezeHeld = rightGamepad?.getButtonPressed(InputComponent.Squeeze);
const thumbstick = rightGamepad?.getAxesValues(InputComponent.Thumbstick);
const aPressed = rightGamepad?.getButtonDown(InputComponent.A_Button);
```

`world.input.xr` is **fully typed** (`XRInputManager`); `.gamepads.left/.right` is
`StatefulGamepad | undefined`. No `as any` is ever needed — if TS complains you are
on a pre-0.4 API; upgrade instead of casting. Two gotchas that silently kill input:

- **All `getButton*`/`getAxesValues` take a string id** (the `InputComponent` enum
  value, e.g. `InputComponent.Thumbstick === 'xr-standard-thumbstick'`). Passing a
  **number** silently returns `undefined` (internal Map miss) — `getAxesValues(2)` is
  a bug, not a stick.
- **`getAxesValues(id)` returns `{ x, y } | undefined`**, never an array. Read
  `.x`/`.y`; `[0]`/`[1]` are always `undefined`.

```typescript
const right = world.input.xr.gamepads.right;
if (right) {
  if (right.getButtonDown(InputComponent.Trigger)) fire(); // edge: pressed this frame
  const stick = right.getAxesValues(InputComponent.Thumbstick); // { x, y } | undefined
  if (stick) aim(stick.x, stick.y); // NOT stick[0]/stick[1]
}
```

Raw WebXR gamepad indices are profile-specific fallback knowledge; prefer
`InputComponent` APIs in app code. Do not transfer raw profile indices directly
to the runtime CLI: `iwsdk xr set-gamepad-state` uses its documented synthetic
schema (`0=trigger`, `1=squeeze`, `2=thumbstick`, `3=A/X`, `4=B/Y`, `5=thumbrest`).

### Anti-pattern: raw WebXR session polling / hand-rolled raycaster

Old example templates poll WebXR directly — delete this when scaffolding from one.
Any `(world as any).renderer?.xr?.getSession()` or `getSession().inputSources` in
input code is a red flag (it bypasses controller-profile remapping and the
interaction system).

```typescript
// WRONG — raw session polling + numeric button indices
const session = (world as any).renderer?.xr?.getSession?.();
for (const src of session.inputSources) {
  if (src.gamepad.buttons[0].pressed) fire();
}
```

For pointing at / clicking objects, do **not** raycast by hand. Tag the target entity
with `RayInteractable` (or `PokeInteractable` for touch); the `InputSystem` registers
it as a target and adds transient `Hovered`/`Pressed` tags you query in a system:

```typescript
import { RayInteractable, Pressed } from '@iwsdk/core';

entity.addComponent(RayInteractable);
// in a system over { required: [RayInteractable] }:
if (e.hasComponent(Pressed)) handleClick(e);
```

### Controller world pose (ray & grip spaces)

To attach to a controller or read its world transform (gesture detection, saber
attach), go through the ECS space entities — not raw gamepad pose arrays.

```typescript
// playerSpaceEntities values are Entities; the Object3D is the `.object3D`
// PROPERTY (there is no getObject3D() method).
const grip = world.playerSpaceEntities.gripSpaces.right.object3D; // Object3D | undefined
const pos = new Vector3();
grip?.getWorldPosition(pos); // world-space controller position
grip?.add(saber.group); // parent content directly to the grip space
```

Available spaces: `head`, `raySpaces`, `gripSpaces`, `indexTipSpaces`, each
`{ left, right }`. Spaces are only valid in an active XR session — gate on
`world.input.xr` / session state. Do not write 0.3/0.4 OR-chains (`world.player` is
_not_ removed; pick one path, prefer `playerSpaceEntities[...].object3D`).

### Haptics (rumble)

There is no IWSDK haptics helper — drive the raw WebXR actuator.
`world.input.xr.gamepads[hand].gamepad` is the typed raw `Gamepad`, so only the
non-standard `pulse` call needs a cast:

```typescript
function rumble(hand: 'left' | 'right', intensity = 1, durationMs = 60) {
  const gp = world.input.xr.gamepads[hand]?.gamepad; // typed Gamepad, no cast
  const actuator = gp?.hapticActuators?.[0] as
    | { pulse?: (i: number, ms: number) => void }
    | undefined;
  actuator?.pulse?.(Math.min(intensity, 1), durationMs);
}
```

Do not cast the whole gamepad to `any` — `.gamepad` is already typed; only the
experimental `pulse` call needs narrowing.

### Mutating components at runtime

`Entity` exposes `setValue(Component, key, value)` and `getValue` — there is **no**
`setComponentValue` method (calling it silently no-ops). Built-in components read
their fields live each frame, so update in place — do **not** remove + re-add a
component to force a refresh (e.g. `Follower.offsetPosition` is re-read every update;
change it with `setValue`). To show/hide an entity toggle `entity.object3D!.visible` —
never move panels off-screen (`y: -100`) or strip/re-add `Follower`.

### Keyboard (browser-first)

`world.input.keyboard` is **already** a typed `StatefulKeyboard` — never write
`(world.input as any).keyboard`. The methods are fully typed; the cast only
discards type safety. (`keyboard` is on `world.input`, NOT `world.input.xr`.)

```typescript
world.input.keyboard.getKeyPressed('KeyW'); // held this frame
world.input.keyboard.getKeyDown('Space'); // pressed this frame
world.input.keyboard.getKeyUp('KeyE'); // released this frame
```

### Dual-input pattern

For games that should work in both browser and VR:

```typescript
// In your update loop (inside a System), direct browser fallback:
if (this.input.keyboard.getKeyDown('Space')) {
  fire();
}

// For shared fire/select intent, register explicit keyboard and XR action
// bindings, or use the built-in locomotion actions where applicable.
```

## coordinates

Standard Three.js / WebXR conventions apply:

- **Right-handed** coordinate system.
- **-Z is camera forward.** Cameras look along local -Z.
- **+Y is up.**
- The player origin starts at identity; with an unrotated camera, forward is -Z.
- In browser-first mode, the camera starts at the configured position
  (default: `[0, 1.7, 0]` looking toward -Z unless `render.camera` overrides it).

For games with approach lanes (e.g. rhythm games):

- For a lane aligned with camera forward (-Z), spawn far objects at more
  negative Z values.
- Move those objects toward +Z as they approach the player.
- Put the hit zone near the player at a small negative Z (e.g. `z = -2`).

PanelUI is the spatial-UI system. It is a **two-step authoring → runtime flow**, it
loads **asynchronously**, and its elements are **uikit components, not the DOM**.
These three facts cause most UI friction; each is covered below. (There is also a
deeper `iwsdk-ui` skill.)

## Contents

- [Compile pipeline (no CLI — it's a Vite plugin)](#compile-pipeline-no-cli--its-a-vite-plugin)
- [Loads asynchronously — gate on the `qualify` event, not `setTimeout`](#loads-asynchronously--gate-on-the-qualify-event-not-settimeout)
- [Runtime access — uikit components, NOT the DOM](#runtime-access--uikit-components-not-the-dom)
- [uikitml authoring rules](#uikitml-authoring-rules)
- [`PanelUISystem` config](#paneluisystem-config)
- [`config` paths must be relative](#config-paths-must-be-relative)

## Compile pipeline (no CLI — it's a Vite plugin)

You author `ui/*.uikitml`; the `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml` plugin (`compileUIKit()`
in `vite.config.ts`) compiles them to `public/ui/*.json` with an exact 1:1 name map.
`PanelUI` then references the **compiled JSON path**, never the `.uikitml` source.

There is **NO standalone compile command.** Do not run `iwsdk uikit compile`,
`iwsdk ui compile`, or `npx @iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml compile` — none exist
(`iwsdk` only has `adapter|dev|mcp|reference|runtime|status`; the plugin ships no
bin). Never hand-author the JSON.

```ts
// vite.config.ts — order matters: iwsdkDev BEFORE compileUIKit
plugins: [
  mkcert(),
  iwsdkDev({
    /* ... */
  }),
  compileUIKit({ sourceDir: 'ui', outputDir: 'public/ui', verbose: true }),
];
```

Compilation runs **only** inside Vite: at **dev** (initial compile + file watcher →
HMR reload) and at **build** (prints a `🎨 UIKitML Compilation Summary:` listing each
emitted JSON — this summary appears on **build only**, never during dev serve). It
`ensureDir`s the output, so no `mkdir -p public/ui` is needed.

- `[compile-uikitml] Source directory not found: ui` / `No .uikitml files found`
  are **benign** (verbose-only, no error) — they just mean you have no `ui/` yet.
  Do not abandon PanelUI over them; create `ui/<name>.uikitml`.
- `public/ui/*.json` is **build output** — treat it as generated. Some templates
  `.gitignore` it; others commit it. Either way, a missing/misnamed JSON **404s
  silently at runtime** (`loadPanel` does `await fetch(config)` and only
  `console.error`s a 404 — the build still passes and the panel just never appears).
  Symptoms live in `iwsdk browser logs`, not the terminal.

```bash
# Verify every .uikitml has a compiled JSON (any name printed = a panel that will 404):
comm -23 <(ls ui/*.uikitml | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.uikitml$//' | sort) \
         <(ls public/ui/*.json 2>/dev/null | xargs -n1 basename | sed 's/\.json$//' | sort)
# When cloning/reusing a project, delete public/ui/ first so stale orphan JSON
# (a .json with no .uikitml source) can't ship dead UI.
```

## Loads asynchronously — gate on the `qualify` event, not `setTimeout`

`addComponent(PanelUI, { config })` loads the JSON asynchronously and attaches the
`PanelDocument` component (and the queryable `UIKitDocument`) **only after** the load
resolves. So immediately after `addComponent`, `getValue(PanelDocument, 'document')`
is `undefined` for the first frames.

**Do NOT** use `setTimeout(bind, 500/1500/3000)`, accumulated-delta timers, or
per-frame `tryBindUI()` poll loops. There is a deterministic ready signal: the ECS
query **`qualify` event** on a query that requires both `PanelUI` and `PanelDocument`.
It fires exactly once, the frame the document becomes available. This is the
official starter-template pattern:

```ts
import {
  createSystem,
  PanelUI,
  PanelDocument,
  UIKitDocument,
  UIKit,
  eq,
} from '@iwsdk/core';

export class GameUISystem extends createSystem({
  // qualifies only after the async document load completes;
  // scope per-config with `where` when you have multiple panels.
  menu: {
    required: [PanelUI, PanelDocument],
    where: [eq(PanelUI, 'config', './ui/menu.json')],
  },
}) {
  init() {
    this.queries.menu.subscribe('qualify', (entity) => {
      const doc = PanelDocument.data.document[entity.index] as UIKitDocument;
      if (!doc) return; // defensive; qualify implies attached

      const start = doc.getElementById('btn-start') as UIKit.Text;
      start?.addEventListener('click', () => this.startGame());
    });
  }
}

world.registerSystem(GameUISystem);
```

## Runtime access — uikit components, NOT the DOM

`getElementById` returns an untyped uikit `Component<any>`. Narrow it to the concrete
uikit element type and use the **typed uikit API** — do not cast to `any`.

```ts
const doc = entity.getValue(PanelDocument, 'document') as
  | UIKitDocument
  | undefined;

const label = doc?.getElementById('score') as UIKit.Text | undefined;
label?.setProperties({ text: '1200' }); // set text
label?.setProperties({ backgroundColor: '#ff8800', borderColor: '#664400' }); // style

const btn = doc?.getElementById('start') as UIKit.Text | undefined;
btn?.addEventListener('click', () => startGame()); // wire interaction
```

Gotchas (these cause the recurring `as any` and `TS2339` churn):

- These are uikit elements, **not** DOM. `.textContent`, `.innerText`, `.onclick`,
  `.style` do not exist — `tsc` throws `TS2339: Property 'text'/'onclick' does not
exist on type 'Component<any>'`.
- **Mutate via `setProperties({ ... })`.** Do not assign `el.text.value = x` or
  `el.backgroundColor = { value: x }` — those are private signals; they may work at
  runtime but are off the public type, which is exactly why agents reach for `as any`.
- Read computed values via `el.properties.value.<prop>`.
- Selectors: `#id` → `getElementById`, `.class` → `querySelector`. Set the element's
  `name` in uikitml to make it discoverable by id.

Typed helper (drop-in, no `as any`):

```ts
const getDoc = (e: Entity) =>
  e.getValue(PanelDocument, 'document') as UIKitDocument | undefined;
const setText = (e: Entity, id: string, text: string) =>
  (getDoc(e)?.getElementById(id) as UIKit.Text | undefined)?.setProperties({
    text,
  });
```

Canonical reference: `examples/audio/src/panel.ts`,
`examples/environment-raycast/src/panel.ts`.

## uikitml authoring rules

`.uikitml` compiles via `@pmndrs/uikitml` then renders through `@pmndrs/uikit`. The
compile step does **not** validate layout values or glyph coverage — both classes of
error escape the build and only surface at render time in XR. Catch them before
verifying:

**1. ASCII only in panel text** (and in runtime panel strings set from `.ts`). The
default font (Inter MSDF) has limited glyph coverage; emoji, `★ ☆`, `◀ ▶ ⬆`, em-dash
`—`, bullets `• ·`, box-drawing `─` emit `Missing glyph info for character …`
(console-only; build still succeeds) and render as blank/tofu in the headset.

| Want          | Use         |
| ------------- | ----------- |
| `—` (em-dash) | `-` or `--` |
| `← →`         | `<-` `->`   |
| `★ ⭐`        | `*`         |
| `• ·`         | `-` or `*`  |
| `▶ ◀`       | `>` `<`     |

```bash
# Flag any non-ASCII left in panel sources (automate this — manual sweeps miss glyphs):
grep -rnP '[^\x00-\x7F]' ui/*.uikitml src/**/*.ts
```

**2. No multi-value padding/margin shorthand.** `padding: 0.8 2;` or `margin: 0.3 0;`
is **not** supported. `compileUIKit` accepts it and writes JSON, but at render Yoga
throws `Invalid Yoga point: 0.8 2` — a late failure during XR verification, not at
build. Use a single value or explicit per-side props:

```css
/* BAD — throws "Invalid Yoga point" at render */
padding: 0.8 2;
/* GOOD */
padding: 1; /* single value */
padding-top: 0.8;
padding-bottom: 0.8;
padding-left: 2;
padding-right: 2;
```

```bash
grep -rnE '(padding|margin)[a-z-]*:[[:space:]]*[-0-9.]+[[:space:]]+[-0-9.]+' ui/*.uikitml
```

## `PanelUISystem` config

Only `kits` and `preferredColorScheme` are accepted by `PanelUISystem`
(`forwardHtmlEvents` was removed in 0.4.0).

## `config` paths must be relative

Author `config: './ui/x.json'` (relative), **never** `'/ui/x.json'` (root-absolute).
The runtime loads it with a raw `fetch(config)` that Vite `base` does not rewrite, so a
root-absolute path 404s on any GitHub subpath deploy — and on the **default Hatch Space
deploy** the daemon serves only `assets/`, so the path additionally has to be relocated
under `./assets/ui/` at deploy time. This is the single most common deploy break — see
06-build-and-deploy.md for the full treatment (Space relocation + the pre-deploy grep
gate). The scaffold and `examples/*` all author these relative (`./ui/welcome.json`);
keep that style from the start.

tower.position.set(Math.cos(a) * 2.2 + 0, 0, Math.sin(a) * 2.2 - 4);\n    env.add(tower);\n  }\n\n  // Floating decorations\n  for (let i = 0; i < 14; i++) {\n    const x = (Math.random() - 0.5) * item.text: Now let me write the README, update the build log, status file, and summary:

item.name: write
item.arguments:
{"content":"# Neon Launch VR

A rocket launch simulation game built with IWSDK (Immersive Web SDK) for VR and browser play. Control a multi-stage rocket through atmospheric flight to orbital insertion.

## Play

**Live:** [https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-launch/](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-launch/)

## Controls

### Browser
- **W/S or Up/Down** - Throttle up/down
- **A/D or Left/Right** - Adjust trajectory angle
- **Space** - Stage separation
- **ESC/P** - Abort mission

### VR (Quest)
- **Right Thumbstick Y** - Throttle control
- **Right Thumbstick X** - Angle control
- **A Button** - Stage separation
- **B Button** - Abort mission

## Missions

| Mission | Target | Difficulty |
|---------|--------|------------|
| Low Orbit | 200 km | Easy |
| Medium Orbit | 400 km | Easy |
| High Orbit | 800 km | Medium |
| GEO Transfer | 2000 km | Medium |
| Deep Space | 5000 km | Hard |
| Crew Delivery | 400 km | Medium |
| Station Module | 600 km | Hard |
| Escape Velocity | 10000 km | Expert |
| Daily Mission | Random | Daily |

## Features

- **Flight Physics**: Realistic atmospheric drag, inverse-square gravity, throttle/fuel management
- **Stage Separation**: 2 or 3 stage rockets with mass reduction and fuel refill on separation
- **Fuel Types**: Standard, High Thrust (more power, faster burn), Efficient (less power, slower burn)
- **30 Achievements**: Altitude milestones, perfect launches, fuel efficiency, daily streaks
- **8 Rocket Skins**: Unlock through gameplay milestones
- **5 Arena Themes**: Neon Holodeck, Crimson Pad, Toxic Launch, Ultra Violet, Solar Blaze
- **XP/Level Progression**: 50 levels with 20 rank titles
- **Daily Missions**: Date-seeded random targets
- **All UI via PanelUI**: 15 spatial UI templates, zero HTML DOM

## Tech

- IWSDK 0.4.1+ (Three.js / super-three + ECS)
- TypeScript, Vite 7
- 1 source file + 15 uikitml templates
- Dual runtime: VR + Browser
- Procedural audio (Web Audio API): 15+ SFX + ambient drone
- Holodeck environment with neon wireframe aesthetics
","path":"neon-launch/README.md"}

Context checkpoint compaction summary:
## Context Checkpoint: Neon Launch VR Build (PM Cycle, 2026-06-09)

### Project Identity
- **Game:** Neon Launch VR — rocket launch simulation (first rocket/space genre in portfolio)
- **Repo:** `https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-launch` (created and pushed)
- **Pages:** `https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-launch/` (deployed via gh-pages branch)
- **Game dir:** `/home/hatch/workspace/neon-launch`
- **Status file:** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json` (set to `waiting_continuation`, round 1 complete)

### Round 1 Completed (45 min)
- **Files:** 1 src (`src/index.ts`) + 15 uikitml = 16 total
- **Lines:** 1,700
- **tsc --noEmit:** 0 errors
- **Preflight checks:** All 5 passed
- **Vite build:** Success, 15/15 uikitml compiled, 0 failed
- **Screenshot:** Renders correctly — rocket on launch pad with holodeck environment visible
- **Browser logs:** Zero errors

### Key Technical Decisions
1. **Scaffolded manually** (not via `npx @iwsdk/create`) because CDN fetch fails in this environment. Copied `node_modules` from `neon-toss`.
2. **World.create options:** `xr: { offer: 'once'}`, `locomotion: false`, `physics: false` — using custom flight physics, not Havok.
3. **Input pattern:** `const keyboard = (world.input as any).keyboard?? world.input;` and `(world.input as any).xr?.gamepads?.right` — required casts due to local SDK typing.
4. **Vite config:** `server: { host: '0.0.0.0', port: 8081}` — removed `https: true` to fix SSL error.
5. **Playwright:** Symlinked chromium revisions 1208→1217 and 1223→1217 to match plugin expectations.
6. **PanelUI panels:** World-space panels use `createWorldPanel()` with position; follower panels use `createFollowerPanel()` with `Follower` + `FollowBehavior.PivotY` targeting `world.player.head`.

### Features Built (Round 1)
- 2/3-stage rocket geometry (nose cone, body stages, fins, nozzle, exhaust flame + glow)
- Flight physics: exponential atmosphere, inverse-square gravity, drag, thrust/fuel burn, throttle+angle control
- Stage separation (Space/A_Button): mass reduction, fuel refill, thrust reduction
- 3 fuel types: standard, high-thrust, efficient
- 9 missions (Low Orbit 200km through Escape Velocity 10000km + Daily Challenge with seeded PRNG)
- 30 achievements with localStorage persistence
- 8 rocket skins with gameplay-gated unlocks
- 5 arena themes (Neon Holodeck, Crimson Pad, Toxic Launch, Ultra Violet, Solar Blaze)
- XP/Level progression (50 levels, 20 titles from Cadet to NEON GOD)
- Holodeck environment: grid floor/ceiling, launch pad platform with edge ring, 4 support towers, 14 floating decorations, 40 ambient particles
- Procedural Web Audio: 15+ SFX (ignition, thrust, separation, explosion, orbit, warning, countdown, etc.) + ambient drone
- 15 PanelUI templates: title, modes, difficulty, hud, throttle, countdown, gameover, achievements, stats, settings, help, skins, toast, leaderboard, pause
- Particle pool (150), trail renderer, toast notifications, daily streak tracking

### What Remains To Do

1. **Update BUILD_LOG.md** — Round 1 entry for Neon Launch VR needs to be appended after the existing entries (was about to do this).
2. **Write `latest-build-summary-pm.md`** at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/`.
3. **Push updated commits** to main branch (README was added but not committed/pushed yet).
4. **Copy build journal** from `~/agents/` into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo.
5. **Continue building (rounds 2+)** — the game is functional but could be expanded with:
   - Leaderboard system (panel exists but shows "Coming soon")
   - Orbital mechanics visualization (trajectory preview arc)
   - More visual polish (screen shake, camera following rocket, better flame effects)
   - Mission control HUD enhancements (apoapsis/periapsis indicators, G-force display)
   - More game modes (Free Flight, Time Trial, Accuracy Challenge)
   - More achievements (currently 30, could expand to 50+)
   - Rocket configuration options (payload type affects mass/behavior)
   - Sound improvements (synthwave background music with chord progression)
   - Tutorial system for first-time players

### Critical Constraints (from SKILL.md)
- **All UI via PanelUI** — zero HTML DOM UI elements
- **ECS Systems** for per-frame logic (`world.registerSystem(GameLoopSystem)`)
- **Import Three.js from `@iwsdk/core`**, never from `three`
- **`tsc --noEmit` + screenshot** is the verification gate, not just `vite build`
- **Relative config paths** for PanelUI: `'./ui/x.json'` not `'/ui/x.json'`
- **ASCII-only** in uikitml templates and runtime strings
- **No multi-value padding/margin shorthand** in uikitml
- Status file must be maintained at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json` with rounds array updated each continuation.

## 2026-06-09 AM -- Neon Toss VR

### Round 1 (Midnight Build Kickoff)
**Date:** 2026-06-09 | **Time:** 07:00-07:45 UTC (45 min)
**Files:** 1 src + 15 uikitml = 16 total | **Lines:** 2,203
**Commits:** 2 | **Deploys:** 1

**Concept:** Holodeck VR ring toss carnival game — first ring toss/carnival genre in the portfolio. Throw neon energy rings at arrays of pegs at varying distances. Rings must encircle pegs (loop around the cylindrical peg body) to score — a fundamentally different mechanic from all prior throwing games (darts hit a board, basketball enters a hoop, bowling rolls to pins, skee-ball launches up a ramp). The ring physics involves torus flight with gravity, angular momentum spin, peg bounce reflections, and encirclement detection based on 2D proximity + height + descent velocity.

**Key Work:**
- Full IWSDK 0.4.1 dual-runtime (VR + browser) with `xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `canvasPointerEvents`
- 9 scoring pegs across 4 distance rows:
  - Row 1 (2m): 3 pegs, 10 pts each, 0.04m radius
  - Row 2 (3m): 3 pegs, 25 pts each, 0.035m radius
  - Row 3 (4m): 2 pegs, 50 pts each, 0.03m radius
  - Row 4 (5m): 1 golden peg, 100 pts, 0.025m radius
- Peg geometry: cylinder body + base disc + wireframe edges + tip sphere + additive glow sphere + point light (50+ pt pegs)
- Ring torus physics:
  - Gravity (-6.0 m/s^2), forward velocity (3-8 m/s based on charge power), upward arc (2-4 m/s)
  - Tumble rotation (random angular velocity on X and Z axes)
  - Peg collision: 2D distance check + height check + descent velocity gate
  - Encirclement: ring within 0.12m of peg center horizontally, below peg tip height, descending — settles flat at peg base
  - Peg bounce: side-hit detection with velocity reflection (0.6 damping), tracks bounced state for achievement
  - Floor landing with sound effect, auto-cleanup after 1s
  - Out-of-bounds removal (z<-8, y<-2, |x|>5)
- Charge-and-release throw mechanic: hold Space/trigger to charge (0-100%), release to throw with power-mapped velocity
- Aim control: A/D keys or right thumbstick adjust horizontal aim, ring start position offset by aimX
- 8 game modes:
  - Classic: fixed rings (15/10/7 by difficulty), highest score
  - Speed: timed (60/45/30s), unlimited rings
  - Target: highlighted peg gives 2x bonus, re-picks on hit
  - Distance: fixed rings, focus far pegs
  - Trick Shot: 20 rings, track special tricks
  - Daily Challenge: 10 rings, date-seeded PRNG layout
  - Survival: 90/60/45s timer, 3 misses = game over
  - Practice: unlimited, no pressure
- 3 difficulty levels: Easy/Medium/Hard affecting ring count, timer duration
- Combo scoring: consecutive ringers build multiplier x1 to x10, 2.5s decay timer, points = base * min(combo, 10)
- 40 achievements with localStorage:
  - Ringer milestones (1/10/50/100/500), score thresholds (500/1K/5K/10K), combo tiers (3/5/8/10)
  - Golden ringer, double ringer, bounce ringer, accuracy (80%/100%), perfect game
  - Speed 10, distance 50/100, daily done/3-day/7-day, survival 30s/60s
  - Trick shots (3/all 6), games (10/50/100), all modes, fashionista, theme tourist
  - Level milestones (10/25/50), career totals (10K/50K)
- 8 ring skins with gameplay-gated unlocks: Neon Cyan (default), Solar Flare (50 ringers), Plasma Pink (5K score), Frost Ring (10 games), Toxic Green (x5 combo), Royal Gold (perfect game), Void Purple (80% accuracy), Inferno (all modes)
- 5 arena themes: Neon Holodeck (cyan), Crimson Arena (red), Toxic Neon (green), Ultra Violet (purple), Solar Blaze (orange) — each defines grid, accent, bg, fog, wall, peg, ring, glow colors
- XP/Level progression: 50 levels with scaling thresholds (100 + 50*level), 20 titles (Novice through Omega), XP from score/10 + hits*5
- **ALL UI via PanelUI** — 15 `.uikitml` templates, zero HTML DOM:
  - Title (world-space: PLAY, SCORES, ACHIEVEMENTS, STATS, RING SKINS, SETTINGS, HELP + level display)
  - Mode Select (world-space: 8 mode buttons + BACK)
  - Difficulty (world-space: Easy/Medium/Hard + BACK)
  - HUD (Follower: score, rings left, hits, combo, time, mode)
  - Power Bar (Follower: 10-segment charge indicator)
  - Pause (world-space: RESUME + QUIT)
  - Game Over (world-space: mode, score, rings, accuracy, combo, REMATCH + MENU)
  - Leaderboard (world-space: 10-row with rank/score/mode/date)
  - Achievements (world-space: 15 per page with pagination)
  - Settings (world-space: master/SFX/music volume +/-, theme prev/next)
  - Stats (world-space: 10 career statistics + level)
  - Skins (world-space: 2x4 grid of 8 skins with status)
  - Help (world-space: browser/VR controls, scoring, all 8 modes)
  - Countdown (Follower: 3-2-1-TOSS!)
  - Toast (Follower: 2s auto-dismiss notifications)
- Aim guide: 30-dot predictive arc trajectory with additive blending, opacity fade with distance
- Particle pool: 150 pre-allocated particles with gravity, color-coded bursts (20 on ringer, golden gets special burst)
- Procedural Web Audio: throw (triangle+sine), ringer (4-note ascending arpeggio), miss (dual descending sawtooth), bounce (square+triangle), golden (5-note ascending arpeggio), combo (escalating triangle pair), countdown tick, go, click, achievement (5-note ascending), levelup (6-note ascending), land (sine+noise), charge (rising sine), gameStart (4-note ascending), gameOver (4-note descending) + arpeggiator (Am cycling) + ambient drone (55Hz sine + 82.5Hz triangle + 110Hz sine + LFO)
- Volume controls: master, SFX, music with +/- in settings, localStorage persistence
- Ring visuals: torus mesh + wireframe edges + additive glow torus, skin-colored
- Peg glow pulse: sinusoidal opacity animation, target mode red highlight with 4x speed pulse
- Holodeck environment: neon grid floor/ceiling (20x20), 14 floating wireframe decorations (torus/box/sphere/cone) with rotation + vertical bob, 40 ambient particles with drift + opacity pulse, 2 accent point lights + directional + ambient, fog
- Throw line: neon accent marker on ground at z=-0.5
- XR controller input: right thumbstick aim, trigger charge/throw, B pause, laser pointer for menus
- Browser input: A/D aim, Space charge/throw, ESC/P pause, R rematch

- Comprehensive README with controls, scoring, modes, tech stack

## 2026-06-07 AM -- Neon Slicer VR

### Round 1 (Midnight Build Kickoff)
**Date:** 2026-06-07 | **Time:** 07:00-07:45 UTC (45 min)
**Files:** 1 src + 15 uikitml + 2 config TS = 18 total | **Lines:** 2,820
**Commits:** 1 | **Deploys:** 1

**Concept:** Holodeck VR slicing game -- first slicing/cutting genre in the portfolio. Wield dual neon energy blades (one per VR controller) to slice through flying wireframe objects mid-flight. Objects launch from below in parabolic arcs, player swings controllers to cut them. Speed-based slice detection via line-segment/sphere intersection. Objects split into two glowing halves that drift apart with physics. Completely unique mechanic -- dual-hand blade tracking with velocity-gated cutting -- distinct from all prior hit/throw/aim games.

**Key Work:**
- Full IWSDK 0.4.1 dual-runtime (VR + browser) with `xr: { offer: 'once' }` + `canvasPointerEvents`
- Dual energy blade system: blade meshes (shaft + glow cylinder + handle + tip sphere) attached to each VR grip space. In browser, single blade follows mouse position raycasted to z=-1.8 plane
- Speed-based slice detection: each frame tracks blade tip positions (previous + current), computes tip velocity. If speed > 2.0 m/s, performs line-segment/bounding-sphere intersection test against all active objects. Uses quadratic discriminant for precise intersection
- 6 object types with distinct Three.js geometries and scoring:
  - Cube (BoxGeometry, cyan, 100pts)
  - Sphere (SphereGeometry, green, 150pts)
  - Diamond (OctahedronGeometry, magenta, 200pts)
  - Star (ConeGeometry, gold, 300pts)
  - Bomb (IcosahedronGeometry, red, -500pts, lose life)
  - Freeze (TorusKnotGeometry, blue, 50pts, 5s slow-motion)
- Object split effect: on slice, original hides, two half-meshes spawn offset along slice normal, drift apart with velocity + rotation + gravity + opacity fade over 1.2s
- 6 game modes:
  - Classic (10 waves, 3 lives, progressive difficulty)
  - Zen (no bombs, no pressure, endless flow)
  - Time Attack (60 seconds, maximize score)
  - Survival (increasing speed, 3 lives)
  - Frenzy (30s rapid fire, no bombs)
  - Daily Challenge (date-seeded PRNG, 15 waves, same puzzle globally)
- 3 difficulty levels (Easy/Medium/Hard) affecting object speed, bomb frequency, hitbox tolerance
- Combo system: consecutive slices within 1.8s build multiplier x1-x10, points = base * (combo+1)
- Slow-motion: slicing freeze orbs halves game speed for 5 seconds (visual + physics slow, real-time countdown)
- Wave system: mode-dependent spawning -- Classic/Daily use wave-clear gating, Zen/TimeAttack/Frenzy use interval spawning, Survival uses decreasing intervals
- 30 achievements with localStorage: slice milestones (1/10/50/100/500), combo tiers (x3/x5/x8/x10), score thresholds (1K/5K/10K/25K), streak (10/25 without miss), bomb dodge (10 in game), freeze (5 in game), accuracy (80%/95%), games (10/50), mode-specific (Classic win, Survival 60s, Frenzy 1K, Daily done, Zen 100), dual slice (2 simultaneous), speed slice (5 in 2s), skin unlock, theme explorer
- 8 blade skins with gameplay-gated unlocks: Neon Cyan (default), Inferno (50 slices), Plasma (5K score), Solar (10 games), Frost (x5 combo), Toxic (classic win), Void (80% accuracy), Rainbow (all modes)
- 5 arena themes: Neon Holodeck (cyan), Crimson Arena (red), Toxic Neon (green), Ultra Violet (purple), Solar Blaze (orange) -- each defines grid, accent, bg, fog, wall colors
- Career stats: games, totalSlices, bestScore, totalScore, bestCombo, totalBombs, totalMisses, totalShots, playTime, modesPlayed, themesUsed
- Top 20 leaderboard (score, mode, slices, combo, date)
- **ALL UI via PanelUI** -- 15 `.uikitml` templates, zero HTML DOM:
  - Title (world-space: 7 buttons), Mode Select (world-space: 6 modes + back), Difficulty (world-space: 3 levels + back), HUD (Follower: score/combo/lives/time/slices/best), Combo (Follower: large combo display), Pause (world-space: resume/quit), Game Over (world-space: score/slices/combo/accuracy/misses/bombs/rematch/menu), Leaderboard (world-space: 10-row), Achievements (world-space: 15 per page + pagination), Settings (world-space: master/SFX/music volume + theme), Help (world-space: controls/gameplay/scoring/modes), Toast (Follower: auto-dismiss), Countdown (Follower: 3-2-1-SLICE!), Skins (world-space: 8 blade skins with status), Stats (world-space: 10 career stats)

item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in (PM Cycle) — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job for the **PM build cycle**. Your job is to check whether the afternoon IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The noon job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
PROJECT="$PWD"
cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
```

Now I have the complete picture. Let me plan the Round 2 improvements:

2. **Synthwave arpeggiator music** - replace the simple drone with a proper chord progression
3. **Screen shake** on stage separation and crashes
4. **G-force and altitude progress display** in HUD
5. **More achievements** - expand to 45
6. **Pause system** - wire the pause panel properly
7. **Altitude visual effects** - stars at high altitude, atmosphere rush particles
8. **Improved exhaust effects** - multi-particle exhaust trail
9. **Score popup system** - floating score on mission complete
10. **Leaderboard panel redesign** with actual data

Now I'm ready to implement these changes. I need to redesign the leaderboard, HUD, and pause panels with proper functionality, then update the source code to handle leaderboard persistence and display.

I'm also adding the synthwave arpeggiator music, screen shake mechanics, G-force readout integration, expanding the achievement system to 45 total, properly implementing pause state, altitude visual effects, and wiring everything through the UI system.

Let me write out all these updates now.
